meaning: adj.谄媚的,奉承的,拍马屁的
examples: Barrow was positively obsequious to me until he learned that I too was the son of a labouring man.
They were obsequious and servile and did not presume to talk to their masters as if they were their equals.
He is a worker who is obsequious to the boss.
In years past, the formula was multitudes of obsequious staff and a heavy handed management style.
In fact, capitalism entails neither robber barons nor kings nor obsequious serfs but independent beings who trade the products of their effort to mutual advantage.
